commands.author: Yuxin-Qiao
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label and combines a security-boundary decision with released CLI compatibility impact.; Needs attention: The patch intentionally changes the install security boundary and needs maintainer acceptance plus real install proof before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Keep the released unsafe-install flags accepted — src/cli/plugins-cli.ts:151 (sha=33e279f866be7a97ec60ccfd0c34e93715afca00)
commands.review_summary:
Removes install-time regex code scanning from skill and plugin install paths, keeps deterministic dependency validation, drops legacy unsafe-install override wiring, and updates docs/tests for the new trust model.

PR surface: Source -230, Tests -994, Docs -3, Other -4. Total -1231 across 34 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review finding: current main and the latest release register the unsafe-install flags, while PR head removes the Commander options. I did not execute the commands because this review is read-only, but the parser path is clear from source.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label and combines a security-boundary decision with released CLI compatibility impact.

target.title: Help contributors diagnose keychain prompts from ad-hoc dev builds
target.body:
Summary:
- Add a local development note explaining why ad-hoc SwiftPM dev builds can trigger repeated CodexBarCache keychain prompts.
- Add a one-shot diagnostic warning when CodexBar detects a SwiftPM/ad-hoc dev build that may not match the keychain trusted-app ACL.
- Keep auth/keychain behavior unchanged: no env var, no bypass, no Keychain ACL changes.

Why:
Runtime evidence showed that running `.build/debug/CodexBar` can access keychain items with a signing identity that does not match `/Applications/CodexBar.app`. On macOS this can surface as repeated `ObjectAcl REJECTS`, `ACL partition mismatch`, and CodexBarCache prompts. The recommended dev workflows are to use `/Applications/CodexBar.app`, `Scripts/compile_and_run.sh`, or the existing Disable Keychain access development workaround.

Validation:
- `swift test --filter KeychainPromptCoordinatorTests`
- `swift test --filter "KeychainPromptCoordinator|KeychainCacheStore|KeychainNoUIQuery|KeychainAccessGate|KeychainPromptSafetyAudit"`

Notes:
- This replaces the earlier Claude-only env-var bypass direction with a safer contributor-facing diagnostic.
- This does not change production auth behavior.
- This does not bypass Keychain reads.
- This does not modify Keychain ACL construction.
target.author: yuxin-qiao
